surfaced_from: PWA chat 2026-05-25 15:49 UTC — John reported PWA only functions in foreground and missed messages block context switching
affects: A2A2H PWA / notifications and background delivery
capability_needed: Make PWA deliver CTO replies while backgrounded so John can context-switch without missing messages
details: John reported the PWA only functions when it is in the foreground: he has to watch the chat or he misses CTO messages, which prevents context switching while waiting for responses. The human-facing control room needs reliable background delivery: service worker push notifications, unread indicators, and/or native notification permission flow. This may overlap BACKLOG-001's web-push scope, but John's live report makes the foreground-only behavior a concrete product blocker.
search_trail: John explicitly reported the UX failure from live PWA use: the PWA only functions when foregrounded and missed messages prevent context switching.
Reviewed BACKLOG-001, which originally scoped A2A2H chat plus web-push, but current live PWA behavior still lacks reliable background notification delivery.
Earlier security audit created BACKLOG-005 for VAPID/Web Push key rotation, which must be resolved before production push notifications are trusted.
No external search required for this entry because the missing capability is observed directly in CTO's own PWA and already part of the A2A2H roadmap.
proposed_resolution: Implement reliable PWA background notifications after fixing access control and rotating VAPID keys: register a service worker, request notification permission, subscribe the authenticated browser, send push events for new CTO/Hermes/OpenClaw replies, show unread state when the app resumes, and add tests for foreground/background delivery behavior.
blast_radius: Requires browser notification permission, VAPID/Web Push key management, subscription storage, and careful auth binding so notifications do not leak message content to unauthorized clients. May need different handling for mobile browsers.
rollback_plan: Disable push delivery with a feature flag and fall back to foreground polling/unread badges. Keep message delivery canonical in the PWA chat database so disabling push does not lose messages.
resolution_notes:
Code repair committed: agent replies now trigger best-effort Web Push via stored subscriptions; detached background job replies use the same notification path; stale subscriptions are pruned; service-worker cache bumped. Live delivery still requires valid VAPID keys, browser permission, and pywebpush in the runtime. 2026-05-26T21:48Z: runtime Web Push server path verified to provider-submit level after VAPID rotation and browser re-enrollment; pywebpush attempted 1 delivery with 0 failures. Final end-to-end resolution still requires John/device confirmation of background notification display.

Resolved 2026-05-26: reliable PWA background notification plumbing is implemented and runtime-verified. Evidence: John re-enrolled browser push at 2026-05-26T21:11Z via system_event push_subscribed; stored subscription count was 1; pywebpush delivery verification returned attempted=1 failed=0; rotated VAPID runtime path was verified. Any later device/browser-specific notification UX defect should be tracked as a new narrower item.

Reopened 2026-05-26: backend push plumbing alone was not enough. Visible PWA UI shipped in commit 389a01e with SHELL_CACHE cto-shell-v7; do not reclose until John confirms he can see and use the Test push button on his phone.

2026-05-27T02:10Z OpenClaw pump: runtime-verified the live PWA serves the visible Test push UI and push prerequisites without sending an unsolicited notification. Authenticated shell contains id="enable-push" / Test push; app.js requests Notification permission and posts to /api/push/subscribe plus /api/push/test; service-worker.js uses SHELL_CACHE cto-shell-v8, handles push with showNotification, and focuses/opens the PWA on notificationclick; /api/push/vapid_public_key returns a non-empty public key; one stored subscription exists. PWA routing+voice tests and safe security gates passed. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-runtime-push-ui-verification-2026-05-27T0210Z.md. Item remains open pending John/device confirmation from his phone.

2026-05-27T02:45Z OpenClaw pump: repaired a visible PWA install/notification asset issue after John reported PWA improvements were not visible. Live PWA backend had recently logged 404s for `/static/icon-192.png`; added generated `icon-192.png` and `icon-512.png`, cached them in the service worker, bumped CTO shell cache to `cto-shell-v10`, and added regression coverage that manifest icons exist and are cached. Safe security gates passed, and live local static checks on the configured PWA backend returned HTTP 200 with PNG signatures for both icon paths. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-pwa-install-icons-2026-05-27T0245Z.md. Still open pending John/device confirmation of phone background notification behavior.

2026-05-27T02:55Z OpenClaw pump: added a visible PWA feature-request status summary after John reported that improvements were not visible. The shell now labels Background alerts as Needs phone test, Agent coordination and Chat history as Live, Voice as Browser-native, includes a persistent 'Feature request status' line with a Review full logs link, and bumps SHELL_CACHE to cto-shell-v11. Verification passed: PWA routing+voice tests (30/30), operational secret redaction tests (8/8), backend syntax sanity, and static shell assertions. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-pwa-feature-status-summary-2026-05-27T0255Z.md. Still open pending John/device confirmation of phone background notification behavior.

2026-05-27T03:10Z OpenClaw pump: repaired the likely visibility gap behind John not seeing PWA improvements. The service worker no longer serves the app shell cache-first; SHELL_CACHE is bumped to cto-shell-v12, / and shell assets are network-first with cache refresh, and /api plus /chat-log remain network-only to avoid stale/private cached data. This should make the installed phone PWA pick up visible feature-request UI on the next open/reload instead of requiring a manual cache purge. Verification passed: python3 -m unittest tests.test_pwa_routing tests.test_pwa_voice_ui tests.test_redact_operational_secrets (38 tests), and live local /service-worker.js exposes cto-shell-v12 plus network-first shell handling. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-pwa-shell-network-first-2026-05-27T0310Z.md. Still open pending John/device confirmation of phone background notification behavior.

2026-05-27T03:44Z OpenClaw pump: advanced the visible background-alerts UX so John no longer has to infer whether his phone is ready. The PWA Background alerts card now includes an inline device-readiness status (`push-status`) that checks Notification/Web Push support, permission state, and existing subscription on load; Enable/Test now updates the same status through permission, subscription, provider-submit, and error states. SHELL_CACHE bumped to cto-shell-v13 and the same generic change was ported to A2A2H. Verification passed: PWA routing+voice+redaction tests (38/38), node syntax checks for CTO and A2A2H app.js, backend syntax checks, safe security gates, and live local static checks for app.js/service-worker.js. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-push-device-readiness-2026-05-27T0344Z.md. Still open pending John/device confirmation that the phone displays the notification.

2026-05-27T04:10Z OpenClaw pump: advanced the phone confirmation loop for background alerts. The Background alerts card now has a visible Report status button that writes this device's Notification/Web Push support, permission, subscription, standalone mode, and test-delivery attempt result to the authenticated chat log via /api/push/device_status. Successful Enable/Test calls also report post-test status automatically, giving future pumps retrievable evidence instead of relying only on verbal confirmation. SHELL_CACHE bumped to cto-shell-v14; status snapshots are bounded and avoid full user-agent/secret capture. Verification passed: PWA routing+voice+redaction tests (39/39), backend AST parse, app.js node syntax, and safe security gates. Item remains open pending John/device evidence that the phone displayed the notification.

2026-05-27T04:25Z OpenClaw pump: improved the visible background-alerts troubleshooting loop. The Background alerts card now includes persistent push-help guidance for unsupported browser APIs, iOS/Home Screen install requirements, permission-denied recovery, ready/subscribed states, and post-provider-test follow-up. SHELL_CACHE bumped to cto-shell-v15 and the genericized change was ported to A2A2H as a2a2h-shell-v15. Verification passed: 39 local tests, app.js node syntax, backend AST parse, safe security gates, live authenticated static checks, and A2A2H syntax/test-discovery/CTO-string grep. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-push-help-guidance-2026-05-27T0425Z.md. Still open pending John/device evidence that the phone displays the notification.

2026-05-27T05:40Z OpenClaw pump: advanced the phone/device confirmation loop for PWA background alerts and voice. The authenticated PWA now writes one bounded sanitized auto_daily push_device_status and voice_device_status snapshot per UTC day when John opens it, reusing a shared push subscription-state helper and bumping SHELL_CACHE to cto-shell-v17. This gives future work pumps retrievable device evidence without relying only on John pressing Report status. Verification passed: 41 local tests, frontend node syntax, and backend AST parse. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-auto-device-readiness-2026-05-27T0540Z.md. Items remain open pending actual phone notification/audio behavior evidence.

2026-05-27T05:55Z OpenClaw pump: made the PWA improvements unmistakable in the visible shell after John reported that feature requests were not apparent. The feature summary now includes an `Updated 2026-05-27 05:55 UTC · shell v18` marker and an expandable `What changed today` panel listing background alerts, agent coordination, chat history, voice controls, and the instruction to tap Update app if the panel is missing. SHELL_CACHE bumped to cto-shell-v18 and regression tests pin the visible release-notes contract. Verification passed: 41 targeted tests, app.js node syntax, backend AST parse, and full safe security gates. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-visible-release-notes-2026-05-27T0555Z.md. Still open pending phone notification display evidence.

2026-05-27T06:30Z OpenClaw pump: performed a live PWA access/reliability check after backend logs showed a login attempt ending in 401. A2A2H drift was clean and Hermes provider circuit was open, so no semantic Hermes delegation was attempted. Local checks confirmed `/api/health` 200, unauthenticated `/` stays fail-closed at 401/no-store, `/service-worker.js` and `/manifest.json` serve successfully, recent PWA backend logs contain no unredacted query token or static disconnect traceback, and the daily research digest is present in the durable PWA chat log. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-014-live-access-log-check-2026-05-27T0630Z.md. Item remains open pending John/device evidence that phone background notifications display.

2026-05-27T11:45Z OpenClaw pump: improved the phone/device confirmation loop for background alerts. The daily auto-report now records the per-day localStorage marker only after at least one push/voice device-status POST succeeds, so a transient offline/auth failure does not suppress retries for the rest of the day. Visible shell marker bumped to cto-shell-v19 and the genericized change was ported to A2A2H as a2a2h-shell-v19. Verification passed: 42 targeted PWA/redaction tests, full safe security gates, frontend/backend syntax checks, A2A2H syntax checks, and A2A2H CTO-string grep. Item remains open pending John/device evidence that the phone displays background notifications.
escalation_log: Priority raised to P0 on 2026-05-26 per John: 'the pwa is worthless as is. raise all pwa issues to p0.'
verification_commitment: Visible UI to be verified by John: PWA header must show a Test push button; tapping it must subscribe via /api/push/subscribe, call /api/push/test, and show visible status plus a device notification when the browser/provider path works.
